couplings.notes: Worn piston rings increase blowby; crankcase overpressure forces oil past worn seals into breather.
Oil consumption reduces sump capacity; low level activates annunciator and starves bearings.
Below safe oil level, main bearings suffer film breakdown and scoring accelerates.
Scored bearing journals increase clearance; oil pump must work harder to maintain pressure.
Increased ring-to-bore gap allows combustion gas bleed; crankcase pressure rises during power.
Elevated crankcase overpressure forces oil past crankshaft seals faster.
Oil viscosity drops nonlinearly with temperature; cold oil resists flow, hot oil films thin.
Sustained oil temperature above 210F accelerates oxidation; acid buildup corrodes bearing journals.
High TAN (acid number) causes etching corrosion on hard bearing surfaces.
Cylinder head temperature rises nonlinearly with power; higher power = faster combustion and wall heating.
Rapid CHT drop after high-power flight thermally stresses exhaust valves; stress cracks initiate at guide interface.
Leaning mixture past peak EGT increases peak cylinder temperature and narrows thermal margin to detonation.
Excessively lean mixture promotes autoignition after flame front; shock waves damage pistons and rings.
Detonation shock loads etch crown and can crack skirt; repeated episodes cause catastrophic failure.
Sustained high-power operation thermally cycles exhaust valves; creep and fatigue reduce margin to burnthrough.
Burned or warped exhaust valve seated improperly; hot gas escapes during compression, power and efficiency drop.
Cylinder with >15pct compression loss misses in idle range; rough running and mag-check drop flag compression loss.
Elevated crankcase pressure blows oil mist past rings onto spark plug electrodes; fouling increases mag-check drop.
Worn intake valve guides leak oil past valve stem into combustion chamber; contributes to oil burn and plug fouling.
Rapid CHT excursions from lean/hot to rich/cool thermally stress piston; micro-cracking initiates in stress concentrators.
Timing drift >3 deg retards spark; combustion deteriorates, roughness increases, power loss becomes noticeable.
Hard landings compress oleo shafts beyond design envelope; seal wiper edges fray and extrude; leak path opens.
Oleo fluid loss reduces shock absorption; subsequent landings transfer more energy to airframe.
High-impact landing loads sidewalls and tread asymmetrically; flat spotting and delamination initiate.
Sustained high-energy braking raises disk temperature; friction coefficient drops (fade), stopping distance increases.
Brake fade >30pct reduces stopping power; pilot may overrun runway on short-field landing.
Sideways force at touchdown flexes tire sidewall; thermal stress and abrasion accelerate cracking and chunking.
Flat-spotted tire creates once-per-rotation imbalance; landing gear mounts and fuselage resonate; fasteners loosen.
Mains-first landing is nominal; oleo absorbs load within design. Nose-wheel or tail-first landing bypasses oleo.
Tail-wheel strike loads aft fuselage; skin crumples, longerons may yield, structural integrity compromised.
Bounced landing unloads then reloads gear at higher velocity; second impact energy can exceed design limit.
Magnesium engine fasteners corrode in humid salt-air environment; white oxide buildup reduces joint preload.
Corroded fastener loses preload during vibration; base studs back out, cylinder head gasket lifts, compression loss.
Higher sustained alternator load (heavy electrical draw) increases brush-to-commutator friction; brush wear accelerates.
When brush length falls below 3 mm, electrical contact becomes intermittent; voltage regulator output drops.
Discharging battery below 10.5V for >30 min causes lead-sulfate crystals to harden; resistance rises, capacity falls.
High battery resistance during cold start limits current to starter; cranking torque drops, start speed inadequate.
If cranking speed <400 rpm, fuel injection quantity overwhelms combustion; fuel floods cylinders, hard-start.
Bus voltage below 20V for >100ms causes avionics reboot; glass-panel flights lose situational awareness.
AHRS gyros experience g-sensitivity; high-g maneuvering or turbulence increases gyro bias; attitude error accumulates.
Rapid on/off heating cycles in icing conditions thermally stress element leads; resistance increases, heating output fades.
Failed pitot heater leaves probe vulnerable to ice; static pressure error propagates to altimeter and VSI.
Groundwater in fuel tank accelerates steel tank corrosion; rust particles clog injectors and fuel servo.
Rust particles and corrosion byproducts lodge in fuel servo inlet screen and injector nozzles; flow restriction.
Vacuum system failure below 4.5 in H2O prevents gyro spin-up; attitude indicator tumbles.
Insect nest or ice in static port introduces fixed pressure offset; altimeter and VSI errors grow with flight profile change.
Spark plug gap erodes during high-power operation and shock-cooling; gap exceeds ignition energy budget, misfire risk.
Misfiring cylinder loses power; rough running, increased fuel consumption, uneven mag-check drop.
Master switch off but electrical load remains (strobes, avionics); battery discharges rapidly, internal resistance rises.
Over-rich mixture at low power increases fuel burn by 35-40%; combustion products condense on cylinder walls.
Unburned fuel condenses in crankcase, mixes with oil, reducing viscosity and film strength.
Oil dilution >8pct reduces viscosity below bearing design threshold; hydrodynamic film collapses, scoring initiates.
Propeller contact with ground imparts bending moment to crankshaft; runout increases, vibration and bearing loads rise.
Crank runout >5 mils generates harmonic vibration; engine mounts and airframe fasteners resonate, fatigue loads increase.
Excessive engine vibration causes alternator brush to bounce on commutator; arcing and accelerated wear.
Density altitude >5000 ft reduces available power 15-20%; climb performance marginal, hot-weather takeoff margin eroded.
Sustained high-power operation increases combustion temperatures and blowby; ring-to-bore contact forces and wear rate rise.
Overspeed by 200+ rpm produces inertial forces on rod; fatigue cracks can initiate at fillet radii.
Positive g-loading >3G compresses engine mounts; rubber compound degrades, stiffness increases, vibration transmission worsens.
Negative g-maneuver sloshes fuel away from carburetor outlet; engine stumbles or quits if inverted >2 seconds.
VFE overspeed produces flutter loads; aileron skin can dimple or wrinkle; flutter risk and control feel deteriorate.
Taxiing with parking brake applied generates sustained friction, rapid heating, and pad/disk wear.
Tight turn at taxi speed scrubs tire sidewall across tarmac; rubber ablates, tread lip separates.
Sustained rudder pedal at taxi speed overloads tire sidewall; flexing and heat buildup accelerates cracking.

recipes.name: Power Percentage
recipes.computation: Estimate engine shaft power as fraction of rated. Compute effective altitude from manifold pressure and OAT; read (RPM, MP) against cruise power chart (65 hp at 2700 rpm, 23.5 inHg sea-level). Interpolate bilinearly. Redline power ~80 hp at 2700 rpm. Clamp to 0-100.
recipes.notes: Used as multiplier for thermal loads and wear rates throughout engine.

recipes.name: Density Altitude
recipes.computation: DA = PA + 120 * (OAT - ISA_temp_at_PA). ISA lapse rate 2C per 1000 ft. At sea level ISA = 15C. Example: PA 0 ft, OAT 30C => DA = 0 + 120 * (30 - 15) = 1800 ft.
recipes.notes: Inputs to power loss and climb performance calculations.

recipes.name: Shock Cooling Rate
recipes.computation: On power reduction, measure max-to-min CHT drop over 30-second window. Rate = (CHT_max_before - CHT_min_after) / 0.5 min. Detect edge: power_pct drops from >70 to <30 within 10 seconds. During transition, accumulate instantaneous rate. Emit peak rate.
recipes.notes: Shock cooling stress event on exhaust valve stems.

recipes.name: Touchdown Energy
recipes.computation: E = 0.5 * m * v^2. m in slugs = weight_lbs / 32.174. v in ft/s = v_fpm / 60. Capture v_fpm when touchdown detected (ground contact + airspeed >30 kt). Store peak energy over landing sequence (touchpoint to rollout).
recipes.notes: Oleo compression fatigue and wheel damage.

recipes.name: Braking Energy
recipes.computation: Power = (brake_psi_left + brake_psi_right) * airflow_constant * airspeed^2. Integrate power over 60-second window after touchdown. Emit peak integral as pulse energy. Constant ~0.08 for disk area and fluid stiffness.
recipes.notes: Brake fade model relies on accumulated thermal energy per landing.

recipes.name: Start Classification
recipes.computation: Cold: OAT <5C and oil_temp <40C. Warm: 5<=OAT<25 and 40<=oil_temp<60. Hot: oil_temp >=60. Flooded: rpm_peak <400 within 10 sec of starter engagement (insufficient combustion speed). Use simple if-then-else tree.
recipes.notes: Categorizes start stress on fuel system, battery, and starter.

recipes.name: Battery Discharge Event
recipes.computation: Detect falling edge: voltage drops below 10.5V for >30 minutes during flight. Set flag once, hold for 1 flight. Clear on next engine shutdown with bus_voltage >24V. Track cumulative count of discharge cycles.
recipes.notes: Deep discharge triggers battery sulfation model.

recipes.name: Oil Consumption Estimate
recipes.computation: Base 0.01 gph at idle. Add power term: 0.002 * power_pct. Add temp term (Arrhenius): 0.005 * exp(0.03 * (oil_temp - 100)). Add blowby term: 0.8 * ring_blowby_proxy. Sum all terms.
recipes.notes: Drives oil sump depletion and bearing starvation model.

recipes.name: Brake-Specific Fuel Consumption
recipes.computation: BSFC = fuel_flow_gph * 6.7 (gal to lbs) / (power_hp). Estimate power from power_pct and 80 hp redline. Typical range 0.35–0.55. Leaner mixtures reduce BSFC; rich mixtures increase it.
recipes.notes: Proxy for combustion efficiency and mixture quality.

recipes.name: EGO Offset from Peak
recipes.computation: Peak EGT is mixture-dependent; occurs ~50F rich of stoichiometric. Estimate peak from knob position and power setting (chart lookup). Measure live avg EGT. Offset = peak_egt_estimate - live_egt_avg. Negative offset = lean of peak (risk of detonation). Debounce to 10-sec window.
recipes.notes: Lean-of-peak indication for thermal stress and detonation risk.

recipes.name: Valve Thermal Load Accumulator
recipes.computation: Accumulate whenever power_pct > 70 and CHT > 350F. Increment = (CHT - 350) / 50 * dt_hours. Thermal load index is cumulative sum over engine life. Periodically (every inspection) reset if valve service completed.
recipes.notes: Exhaust valve creep and burnthrough model.

recipes.name: Piston Thermal Cycling Stress
recipes.computation: Track max and min CHT during each power change cycle (climb to cruise, cruise to descent, descent to idle). Amplitude = max_cht - min_cht. Cycle count increments when power changes >30 pct. Emit as rolling 10-cycle average.
recipes.notes: Piston stress from rapid thermal cycling.

recipes.name: Ring Blowby Proxy
recipes.computation: During power changes, measure oil pressure droop (drop from baseline). Droop correlates to increased crankcase venting. Proxy = 1.0 + (baseline_psi - current_psi) / 10. Baseline 50 psi at cruise. Cap to [1.0, 5.0]. Also correlates oil temp rise during climb.
recipes.notes: Oil consumption multiplier; also feeds spark plug fouling model.

recipes.name: Mag Timing Drift Indicator
recipes.computation: Timing drift correlates to mag point wear and gap increase. Estimate from mag drop differential: if right mag drop > left by >100 rpm, assume right mag drifting retard. Proxy = mag_drop_delta / 100 * 2 degrees. Clamp to ±5 degrees.
recipes.notes: Ignition timing variation drives power loss and rough running.

recipes.name: Fuel Water Ingestion Flag
recipes.computation: Flag if (rain_detected AND sump_screen_clogs >0 during this flight) OR (humidity >80 pct AND parked_outdoors_days >2 without fuel check). Reset after fuel sump drain and inspection.
recipes.notes: Fuel system corrosion and injector clogging model.

recipes.name: Environmental Corrosion Index
recipes.computation: Score = humidity_pct * (1 + temp_c / 100) * parked_multiplier * sqrt(days_parked + 1). Parked multiplier 2.0 if outdoors, 0.2 if hangar. Emit daily. Resets on aircraft wash or engine dehumidification.
recipes.notes: Magnesium fastener and steel tank corrosion accumulator.

recipes.name: Alternator Load Factor
recipes.computation: Load_factor = battery_charge_current / 60 (rated). Clamp to [0, 1.2]. Sustained >0.8 accelerates brush wear. Rolling 10-minute average.
recipes.notes: Alternator brush wear model.

recipes.name: Bus Voltage Dip Detection
recipes.computation: Capture minimum bus voltage over rolling 5-second window. Flag if min <20V. Emit as event (bus dip detected) with min value.
recipes.notes: Avionics power integrity during start and electrical transients.

recipes.name: AHRS G-Load EWMA
recipes.computation: Exponential-weighted moving average. Alpha = 0.1 (10-second effective window). ewma_new = alpha * g_load + (1 - alpha) * ewma_old. Emit rolling estimate.
recipes.notes: AHRS gyro bias accumulation from sustained g-loading.

recipes.name: Pitot Thermal Cycle Counter
recipes.computation: Increment on each rising and falling edge of pitot_heat signal during icing. Emit rolling count. Reset after pitot element replacement.
recipes.notes: Pitot heater element fatigue from thermal cycling.

events.trigger: OAT < 5C and oil_temp < 40C and starter engaged
events.notes: Cold starts increase magneto point arcing and fuel flooding risk; starter and battery heavily loaded.

events.trigger: Engine shutdown <5 min prior and starter engaged
events.notes: Hot soak vaporizes fuel in lines; engine may flood if fuel flow not leaned aggressively.

events.trigger: Starter engaged >2 times without engine light-off, rpm_peak stays <400
events.notes: Excessive starter cycling with inadequate combustion speed floods cylinders; fuel washes past rings.

events.trigger: Power drops from >70% to <30% in <10 sec; CHT drop >15C/min
events.notes: Rapid descent or power-off landing stresses exhaust valve metallurgy; crack initiation risk high.

events.trigger: ego_offset_from_peak < -5 and power_pct > 50
events.notes: Operating lean of peak at high power increases CHT margin to autoignition; detonation risk.

events.trigger: ego_offset < -10 and density_altitude > 5000 ft and power_pct > 60
events.notes: Combined lean mixture and high-DA performance margins shrink; detonation likely on high-power climb.

events.trigger: Mixture full rich AND power < 20% AND duration > 5 min
events.notes: Over-rich idle loads cylinders with unburned fuel; oil dilution and spark fouling.

events.trigger: oil_temp_c > 115
events.notes: Oil temp above 240F (115C) accelerates oxidation and film collapse; bearing starvation risk.

events.trigger: cht_cyl_max > 220
events.notes: CHT above 220C (430F) risks valve margin; combustion knock and preignition.

events.trigger: airspeed > vfe_limit + 10 kt in landing gear down config
events.notes: VFE overspeed loads control surfaces beyond design envelope; flutter and skin damage risk.

events.trigger: airspeed > vno_limit (90 kt estimated cruise)
events.notes: Cruise overspeed stresses airframe and engine limits; fatigue loading on wing and fuselage.

events.trigger: total_g > 3.5 (acrobatic entry or hard pull-up)
events.notes: High-g maneuver compresses engine mounts and airframe joints; structural fatigue and vibration spike.

events.trigger: total_g < -1.5 (inverted or pitch-down maneuver)
events.notes: Negative g sloshes fuel away from carb; fuel starvation risk if sustained >2 sec.

events.trigger: touchdown_energy > 2500 ft-lbf
events.notes: High-energy landing compresses oleo beyond comfort; wheel and tire damage, airframe shock loads.

events.trigger: Main gear ground contact before nose gear by >0.5 sec AND touchdown_energy < 2500
events.notes: Proper mains-first landing allows oleo to absorb energy; normal wear progression.

events.trigger: Tail contact (aft fuselage y-accel spike) before main gear contact OR nose gear first by >1 sec
events.notes: Tail-first or nose-wheel-first landing bypasses oleo shock absorption; structural damage to fuselage.

events.trigger: crosswind_side_load > 500 lbf at touchdown
events.notes: High crosswind load flexes tire sidewall; thermal stress accelerates cracking and chunking.

events.trigger: Gear unload during landing roll (vertical_accel spike to <-0.5g), then reload within 3 sec
events.notes: Bounce unloads then reloads gear at higher velocity; second impact energy may exceed design limit.

events.trigger: Sustained brake pressure >1200 psi AND skid marks detected AND touchdown_energy > 1500
events.notes: Heavy braking on hard landing can lock wheels; tire skids flat, tread ablates unevenly.

events.trigger: Parking brake applied while groundspeed > 3 kt
events.notes: Dragging parking brake generates sustained friction; brake pad and disk overheat rapidly.

events.trigger: Rudder control deflection > 25 deg at groundspeed > 10 kt, turn radius < 100 ft
events.notes: Tight taxi turn scrubs tire sidewall; rubber ablates, tread edge separates.

events.trigger: Rudder control at extremum (>90% deflection) AND groundspeed > 5 kt for >5 sec
events.notes: Sustained rudder override at taxi speed overloads nosewheel; tire sidewall stress and heat.

events.trigger: Ground contact (altitude = 0, vertical_v > -300 fpm) AND CHT rise <20C over next 10 sec
events.notes: Propeller contact with ground imparts bending moment to crankshaft; post-strike CHT rise delayed if power low.

events.trigger: Static noise floor >threshold on radio (stub for future implementation)
events.notes: Placeholder for atmospheric electrical activity; used to correlate avionics anomalies.

events.displayName: Master Switch Off Under Electrical Load
events.trigger: Master switch off AND battery_current_amps > 10 A in prior 10 sec
events.notes: Master off with active loads forces battery to discharge; sulfation and capacity loss.

events.trigger: Battery voltage falls below 10.5V for >30 min during flight
events.notes: Deep discharge causes lead-sulfate crystal hardening; battery capacity and performance degrade.

events.trigger: Engine rpm > 2800 (redline ~2700 nominal)
events.notes: RPM overspeed stresses connecting rods and crankshaft; fatigue cracks can initiate.

events.trigger: Mag-check differential >150 rpm or single-mag rpm <1100 rpm on run-up
events.notes: Excessive mag drop indicates worn points, gap increase, or timing drift; ignition reliability compromised.

events.trigger: Vacuum pressure falls below 4.5 inH2O for >10 sec during flight
events.notes: Vacuum pump failure prevents gyro spin-up; attitude indicator tumbles, navigation unreliable.

events.trigger: Altitude or VSI trend inconsistent with pitch/power changes; pressure sensor diff >200 inH2O from baseline
events.notes: Static port ice or insect blockage introduces fixed pressure offset; altimeter and VSI unreliable.

events.trigger: Airspeed inconsistent with control input OR airspeed <20 kt in climb (impossible)
events.notes: Pitot ice or blockage freezes airspeed; pilot loses primary airspeed reference.

events.trigger: Aircraft position static (groundspeed ~0) AND outdoors_flag = true for >86400 sec
events.notes: Extended outdoor parking enables corrosion and moisture ingestion; increases maintenance risk.

inspections.notes: Replace oil and filter; check sump for metal particles and water; reset oil burn baseline.
Alternative extended interval; preferred only if oil analysis shows acceptable acid number.
Comprehensive engine and systems inspection per FAR 43 Appendix D; includes compression check, spark plug replacement, mag timing, brake fluid, brake pads.
Annual IA/AP airworthiness check; includes weight and balance, airworthiness directives compliance, flutter inspection.
Magneto Inspect-and-Repair-as-Needed (IRAN) per Textron spec; disassemble, clean, gap points, time mags, run test cell.
Propeller blade dressing and hub inspection; check for cracks, corrosion, and oil seal condition.
FAR 91.207; ELT battery must be replaced when expired or when 50% of shelf life consumed, whichever first.
FAR 91.411 certification every 24 calendar months; includes pitot-static system test, altimeter system test, and RVSM check if applicable.
FAR 91.413 certification every 24 calendar months; includes altitude accuracy check and frequency calibration.
Hydraulic brake fluid hygroscopic; replace every 5 years to prevent moisture absorption and corrosion.
Load-test battery under cold-start condition (50A draw); verify voltage >21V at 25C ambient.
Lycoming O-360-A2D TBO 2000 SMOH (Shaft Mean Operating Hours); engines approaching TBO require major overhaul planning.
